text: Do you know everything you need to know about sunscreen? Is your child fully protected? Are
you fully protected? When it comes to skin cancer you don't want to mess around. Here's some simple
ways to keep yourself and others protected so that you're ready to have fun in the sun. Wear
sunscreen especially if their skin freckles or moles. Remember to slap on sunscreen on places
like your lips, ears, feet, hands, bald spots, and the back of your neck. They're the places you'll
miss but the most likely to burn. Remember SPF 15 or above is the safest and the best way to go.
Try to reapply every 40 minutes especially if you're swimming. Water resistant sunscreens are
the only way to go when you're swimming. Other ways to prevent skin cancer are avoiding tanning,
use extra caution near water, snow, and sand, and wear hats and sunglasses with UVA protection.
Contact epa.gov slash sunwise for more information.
